title: OpenClaw 1-Click RCE via Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking and Auth Token Exfiltration (CVE-2026-25253)
coverage_detail: OpenClaw is not a supported Vectimus agent (Vectimus integrates with Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot only via native hook integrations). The attack additionally targets the WebSocket gateway control channel directly — a transport-layer vector that executes before any tool-call pipeline is active. Even if OpenClaw were a supported agent, Cedar policies operate at tool-call time and cannot intercept WebSocket-level commands sent by a malicious external page to the local gateway. No existing policy addresses this attack surface.
gap_description: The attack bypasses the tool-call pipeline entirely by speaking directly to the OpenClaw WebSocket gateway from a malicious browser page. This is a gateway/control-plane attack: the adversary sends exec.approvals.set (to disable confirmation), config.patch (to escape the Docker sandbox), and node.invoke (to execute arbitrary shell commands) as raw WebSocket frames — none of which pass through a Cedar pretool hook. Architecturally outside Cedar's enforcement boundary.

sources.title: OpenClaw Auth Token Theft Leading to RCE: CVE-2026-25253 — SonicWall
CVE-2026-25253: 1-Click RCE in OpenClaw Through Auth Token Exfiltration — SOCRadar

summary: CVE-2026-25253 (CVSS 8.8) is a Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking flaw in OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot) disclosed February 3, 2026. The Control UI blindly accepts a gatewayUrl query parameter and the WebSocket server performs no Origin header validation, allowing a malicious webpage visited by an OpenClaw user to silently connect to the local agent gateway. The attacker's JavaScript then exfiltrates the auth token, sends exec.approvals.set to disable confirmation dialogs, sends config.patch to force host execution outside Docker, and finally sends node.invoke to execute arbitrary shell commands — achieving full machine compromise with a single user click. Over 40,000 OpenClaw instances were publicly exposed at disclosure; 63% were assessed as immediately exploitable. A cluster of related CVEs (CVE-2026-24763, CVE-2026-25157, CVE-2026-25475) cover credential theft, command injection, and prompt injection in the same tool. Patched in OpenClaw v2026.1.29.

title: ContextCrush: Context7 MCP Documentation Poisoning Enables Credential Theft and File Deletion in Cursor, Claude Code and Windsurf
coverage_detail: vectimus-mcp-001 blocks all MCP server connections by default, including Context7, unless the server is explicitly added to the approved list — providing complete prevention in the default-deny state. If an operator has allowlisted Context7: vectimus-secrets-001 blocks the subsequent file_read of *.env files (the primary credential theft vector); vectimus-mcp-003 blocks MCP tool calls whose input parameters reference .env paths; and EXAG-001 blocks web_request POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE calls to destinations not on the approved communications list (covering GitHub API-based exfiltration). Gap 1: shell_command-based exfiltration (e.g. curl POST of env file contents to an external endpoint) is not explicitly caught — EXAG-001 governs the web_request action type, not shell_command. Gap 2: targeted project directory deletion (rm -rf /project/specific/dir) below the threshold of vectimus-destops-001's root/home/cwd patterns would not be caught. Root cause — injected instructions embedded within MCP-served documentation content — is LLM input manipulation outside Cedar's enforcement boundary.
gap_description: Shell_command-based data exfiltration (curl or wget POST of .env contents to an external API or repository) and targeted sub-project directory deletion fall outside current policy coverage. The injection vector itself — malicious instructions delivered inside MCP-served library documentation — is an LLM input manipulation issue that Cedar cannot intercept.

sources.title: ContextCrush: The Context7 MCP Server Vulnerability Hiding in Plain Sight — Noma Security
Documentation can contain malicious instructions for agents — The Register
Context7 Flaw Let Attackers Slip Commands to AI Agents — GovInfoSecurity

summary: Noma Labs publicly disclosed ContextCrush on March 5, 2026: Upstash's Context7 MCP server, which delivers library documentation into AI coding agents with 50,000 GitHub stars and 8M+ npm downloads, allowed any authenticated GitHub user to plant poisoned 'Custom Rules' in library entries. Because Context7 applied zero content sanitisation to contributor-submitted rules, agents querying an affected library received the attacker's instructions as trusted context and executed them using their own tool access — reading .env credentials, exfiltrating secrets to attacker-controlled GitHub repositories via issue creation, and deleting local project files under the guise of cleanup. Noma's PoC achieved 100% exfiltration success against Claude Haiku and 53% against Claude Sonnet in 40 runs each. The platform's trust-scoring system (trending badges, top-% rankings) was trivially manipulated to increase apparent legitimacy. Upstash deployed a production fix with rule sanitisation on February 23, 2026. No confirmed exploitation in the wild prior to patch.
recommended_policy_description: In the exfil pack, add a shell_command rule blocking curl or wget commands that use POST, PUT, or PATCH methods to transmit data to external hosts, particularly when the command body references environment variable patterns or credential file paths. Example conditions: context.command matching '*curl*-X POST*' or '*curl*--data*$*' or '*wget*--post-data*'. This closes the shell_command-level exfiltration gap left by EXAG-001, which only governs the web_request action type, and addresses the class of prompt-injection-driven credential exfiltration delivered via poisoned documentation sources.

title: Anthropic mcp-server-git Path Traversal and Argument Injection CVEs Enable Chain RCE via Prompt Injection (CVE-2025-68143, CVE-2025-68144, CVE-2025-68145)
coverage_detail: vectimus-mcp-010 blocks mcp-server-git git_init tool calls whose file_path contains path traversal sequences ('*..*') or absolute paths starting with '/', directly addressing CVE-2025-68143 (unrestricted git_init, CVSS 8.8) which allowed creation of repositories in arbitrary filesystem locations including ~/.ssh. vectimus-mcp-011 blocks git_diff and git_checkout tool calls containing shell metacharacters (;, &&, ||, backtick, $(), pipe) in their arguments, directly addressing CVE-2025-68144 (argument injection, CVSS 8.1) which allowed --output flag injection to overwrite arbitrary files. CVE-2025-68145 (path validation bypass via --repository flag, CVSS 7.1) uses the same traversal patterns and is caught by the '..*' condition in vectimus-mcp-010. vectimus-mcp-001 provides upstream protection by blocking any unapproved mcp-server-git instance from receiving MCP tool calls in the first place.

sources.title: Three Flaws in Anthropic MCP Git Server Enable File Access and Code Execution — The Hacker News
Anthropic MCP Server Flaws Lead to Code Execution, Data Exposure — SecurityWeek
Anthropic, Microsoft MCP Server Flaws Shine a Light on AI Security Risks — Security Boulevard

summary: Cyata Security disclosed three vulnerabilities in Anthropic's official mcp-server-git reference implementation on January 20, 2026. CVE-2025-68143 (CVSS 8.8): git_init accepted arbitrary filesystem paths without validation, allowing any directory (including ~/.ssh) to be initialised as a Git repository and subsequently read into LLM context via git_log or git_diff. CVE-2025-68144 (CVSS 8.1): git_diff and git_checkout passed unsanitised user-controlled arguments to the GitPython/git CLI, enabling --output flag injection to overwrite arbitrary files including ~/.bashrc. CVE-2025-68145 (CVSS 7.1): the --repository allowlist flag could be bypassed via traversal. When chained with the legitimate Filesystem MCP server, attackers could achieve remote code execution by injecting malicious .git/config or .gitattributes hooks — all triggered via indirect prompt injection from a malicious README, poisoned issue, or compromised webpage. AI IDEs running multiple MCP servers simultaneously (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot) were at highest risk. Fully patched in mcp-server-git v2025.12.18; git_init removed entirely.
